title: AutoHeal - User Input Protection: Focus Theft Detection & Auto-Recovery
desc: Multi-layer system protecting user focus from CDP/Chrome theft during ask operations. LIVING DOCUMENT: update as each focus-theft incident is investigated and resolved. MANDATORY: all restore paths through InputReadiness pipeline. RESTORE TRIGGER: end of each ask/CDP action boundary only. Watchdog = monitor+notify only.
steps: 0. [MANDATORY] ALL focus restore paths go through InputReadiness.Probe() -- OnProbeSuccess fires zoom+auto-hack. Standalone ForceForegroundWindow without InputReadiness FORBIDDEN (except RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync inner loop).
1. [MANDATORY] Restore trigger = end of each ask/CDP action only. Watchdog = monitor+notify only, no restore.

2. [INVESTIGATION PROCEDURE] When focus theft is suspected: (a) identify which ask action was running (insert/send/attach/wait), (b) find which CDP method that action calls (Page.bringToFront / Browser.setWindowBounds / Runtime.evaluate with .focus()), (c) check IsFocusStealingMethod() covers it, (d) check pre-minimize and post-restore are applied, (e) check if Chrome raced between minimize and CDP call completion. Each ask action has a fixed CDP call pattern -- trace the pattern to find the theft point.

3. [CDP FOCUS-STEALING METHODS] Page.bringToFront (most dangerous -- activates Chrome window at OS level), Target.activateTarget (no-op disabled), Browser.setWindowBounds (restore triggers SetForegroundWindow), Runtime.evaluate with window.focus()/.focus()/.click()/showOpenFilePicker(). IsFocusStealingMethod() and IsFocusRiskExpression() in CdpClient.FocusRiskWatchdog.cs are the gatekeepers -- if a new theft is found, add it here.

4. [KNOWN INSTABILITY] Page.bringToFront: Chrome calls SetForegroundWindow asynchronously AFTER CDP response returns -- pre-minimize fires before CDP call, but Chrome OS activation races the 50ms/200ms delays. Browser.setWindowBounds restore: WaitForWindowStateAsync polls 50ms up to 1-2s, but Chrome may activate before state is confirmed. Runtime.evaluate: JS .focus() calls inside Gemini page (e.g. file input activation) can steal focus without going through IsFocusStealingMethod.
8. Action boundary restore: GuardCdpFocusTheft / LogRestoreFocus -> InputReadiness.Probe() -> RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync
9. RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync: IME+cursor snapshot -> ForceForegroundWindow loop -> OnFocusStealer(AutoBugReport+overlay) -> restore on success
11. [DONE] DismissDialogAsync: 동의 added; called after file-attach + before insert. Gemini copyright consent popup appears after image upload -- dismiss at upload completion.
12. [TODO] FireAutoHeal on 5s restore timeout: AutoBugReport(unrecoverable) + FireAutoHeal(focus-theft, chromeHwnd)
author: claude
